accomplishments: Make hover effects smooth instead of instant
Create professional button and card interactions

do.title: Add hover transitions
steps: Create a new file and save it as transitions.html
Copy and paste the code below
Save and open in browser
Hover over the button and card — smooth transitions!
do.code:
Hover This Card

Watch it lift up smoothly!
do.result: Hover effects are now smooth instead of instant — that's the transition property!

concepts.name: The transition property
concepts.definition: transition: property duration timing-function. Tells the browser to animate between states smoothly. Example: transition: all 0.3s ease.
concepts.analogy: Instead of flicking a light switch (instant), you install a dimmer (smooth transition).

concepts.name: Timing functions
concepts.definition: ease (default, slow-fast-slow), linear (constant speed), ease-in (slow start), ease-out (slow end), ease-in-out (slow start and end).
concepts.analogy: How a car accelerates: ease = normal driving, linear = cruise control, ease-in = slow start fast finish.

concepts.name: What can be transitioned
concepts.definition: Colors, backgrounds, transforms, opacity, shadows, sizes, padding, margins. NOT display or visibility (use opacity instead).
concepts.analogy: You can smoothly change paint color or move furniture, but you can't smoothly make a wall appear/disappear.

understand.keyTakeaway: Add transition: all 0.3s ease to any element, then change properties on :hover for smooth effects.

build.goal: Create cards with multiple transition properties
build.hint: transition: all catches every property change. For performance, list specific properties instead.

challenge.title: Animated Feature Cards
challenge.mission: Create 3 feature cards that scale and glow on hover.
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a colored box-shadow glow effect that matches each card's theme color!

capabilities: Add smooth transitions with the transition property
Control speed with duration
Choose timing functions for different feels
Create professional hover effects on buttons and cards

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.2, you'll learn CSS animations with @keyframes — effects that run continuously without user interaction!

do.title: Create a pulsing button
steps: Create a new file and save it as animations.html
Copy and paste the code below
Save and open in browser
The button pulses and the spinner spins — no hovering needed!
do.result: The button pulses continuously and the spinner rotates — all with CSS animations!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing the pulsing button and spinner.

concepts.name: @keyframes
concepts.definition: Defines animation steps. Use percentages (0%, 50%, 100%) or from/to for start/end. Each step defines the CSS state at that point.
concepts.analogy: A flipbook — each keyframe is a page, and the browser fills in the frames between them.

concepts.name: The animation property
concepts.definition: animation: name duration timing-function iteration-count. Example: animation: pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite. Can also add delay, direction, fill-mode.
concepts.analogy: A movie director telling actors: which scene (name), how long (duration), how to move (timing), how many takes (count).

concepts.name: animation-fill-mode
concepts.definition: forwards = stay at final state. backwards = start at first keyframe. both = apply both. Without fill-mode, element snaps back to original state.
concepts.analogy: After a play, do actors stay in their final positions (forwards) or go back to starting marks (none)?

understand.keyTakeaway: @keyframes defines the animation, animation property applies it. Use infinite for continuous effects, forwards for one-shot animations.

build.goal: Create a fade-in animation for page load
build.hint: Use animation-delay and nth-child to stagger animations for a cascading effect!

challenge.title: Loading Spinner Collection
challenge.mission: Create 3 different loading spinners using CSS animations.
challenge.bonusChallenge: Create a 4th spinner that uses multiple animations on the same element!

capabilities: Define animations with @keyframes
Apply animations with the animation property
Create continuous effects with infinite
Build loading spinners and pulse effects
Stagger animations with delay and nth-child

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.3, you'll learn CSS custom properties (variables) — the key to building maintainable design systems and theme switchers!

accomplishments: Create a theme system with CSS variables
Switch between light and dark modes
Update your entire site by changing a few values

do.title: Create your first CSS variables
steps: Create a new file and save it as css-variables.html
Copy and paste the code below
Save and open in browser
Try changing --primary-color in :root to a different color
Watch everything using that variable update instantly!
do.code:
CSS Variables Demo

What are CSS Variables?

CSS variables let you store values and reuse them throughout your stylesheet.

Why Use Them?

Change one variable, update your entire site. Perfect for themes and design systems!
do.result: You defined colors and values once, then reused them everywhere with var(--variable-name)!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing cards with your custom color scheme.

concepts.name: CSS Custom Properties (Variables)
concepts.definition: Store values you want to reuse throughout your stylesheet. Define with --name: value in :root, use with var(--name) anywhere. Change one value, update everything that uses it.
concepts.analogy: A nickname. Instead of writing someone's full name every time, you use their nickname. CSS variables are nicknames for values.

concepts.name: Theme switching with data attributes
concepts.definition: Define different variable values under [data-theme="dark"] or [data-theme="light"] selectors. Toggle the data-theme attribute with JavaScript to instantly switch all colors.
concepts.analogy: A light switch for your entire website — flip it and every room changes at once.

concepts.name: Design systems with variables
concepts.definition: Organize all your values (colors, spacing, typography, shadows, radii, transitions) as variables. This creates a consistent, maintainable system where everything matches.
concepts.analogy: A master control panel — adjust one dial and every part of the building responds.

understand.keyTakeaway: CSS variables make your code maintainable and your themes switchable. Define once, use everywhere, change in one place.

build.hint: Add transition: background-color 0.3s ease, color 0.3s ease to body and cards for smooth theme switching!

challenge.title: Build a Multi-Theme System
challenge.mission: Create a website with 3 different themes users can switch between, with localStorage persistence.
requirements: Three complete themes (light, dark, and one custom theme)
challenge.starterCode:
Multi-Theme Demo

Click theme buttons to switch!
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a fourth purple/pink theme and make the active button visually highlighted!

capabilities: Create and use CSS custom properties (variables)
Build theme systems with variable scoping
Switch themes dynamically with JavaScript
Create comprehensive design systems
Use variables for colors, spacing, typography, and effects
Save user preferences with localStorage
Build maintainable, scalable CSS

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.4, you'll learn to rotate, scale, skew, and create 3D effects — taking your designs to the next level!

do.title: Transform elements
steps: Create a new file and save it as transforms.html
Copy and paste the code below
Save and open in browser
Hover over each box to see different transforms!
do.code:
CSS Transform Effects

Hover over each box to see the transform!

Rotate
Scale
Skew
Move
Multi
do.result: The transform property lets you rotate, scale, skew, and move elements — all with smooth transitions!

concepts.name: rotate(), scale(), translate()
concepts.definition: rotate(45deg) spins an element. scale(1.3) makes it 130% size. translate(50px, -20px) moves it. Can combine: transform: rotate(15deg) scale(1.2).
concepts.analogy: Giving an object physical instructions — "turn 45 degrees, grow to 130%, move up 20 pixels."​

concepts.name: 3D transforms with perspective
concepts.definition: perspective: 1000px creates 3D space. rotateY(180deg) flips a card. transform-style: preserve-3d enables 3D on children. backface-visibility: hidden hides the back.
concepts.analogy: Looking through a window at objects in 3D space — perspective controls how far away you are from the window.

concepts.name: Why transforms are special
concepts.definition: Transforms don't affect document flow (other elements don't move). They're GPU-accelerated for smooth performance. They don't trigger layout recalculation.
concepts.analogy: Moving a sticker on a whiteboard — everything underneath stays exactly where it was.

concepts.name: transform-origin
concepts.definition: Controls the pivot point for transforms. Default is center. transform-origin: top left makes rotation pivot from the corner.
concepts.analogy: The pin in a pinwheel — it determines what point the element spins around.

understand.keyTakeaway: Transform lets you rotate, scale, skew, and move elements without affecting layout. Combine with transitions for smooth interactive effects.

build.goal: Create a 3D card flip and a hover gallery with transforms
build.hint: The back card starts rotated 180deg so when the container flips, it appears facing forward!

challenge.title: Build an Animated Product Showcase
challenge.mission: Create a product card with multiple transform effects — rotation, scale, and 3D perspective.
requirements: Card rotates slightly in 3D on hover
"Add to Cart" button scales on hover
Price tag with attention-grabbing pulse animation
challenge.starterCode:
SALE

Amazing Product

This is the best product you'll ever own.

9.99
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a "Quick View" modal that appears when clicking the image!

capabilities: Use transform functions (rotate, scale, skew, translate)
Combine multiple transforms
Create 3D card flip effects
Use perspective for depth
Build interactive hover transformations
Control transform-origin
Create product showcases with advanced effects

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.5, you'll learn to add shadows, blur, brightness, and other visual effects to create depth and polish!

accomplishments: Use filters to blur, brighten, and adjust images

do.title: Master box-shadow
steps: Create a new file and save it as shadows-filters.html
Copy and paste the code below
Save and open in browser
See how different shadows create different levels of depth!
do.code:
Box Shadow Examples

Subtle

Cards and containers

Medium

Interactive elements

Strong

Strong elevation

Colored

Brand personality

Layered

Multiple shadows

Inner

Inset effect
do.result: Box-shadow adds depth and elevation, making flat designs feel three-dimensional!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing the different shadow styles.

concepts.name: box-shadow
concepts.definition: Syntax: box-shadow: horizontal vertical blur spread color. Creates depth. Use multiple shadows (comma-separated) for realistic layered effects. Add "inset" for inner shadows.
concepts.analogy: A light source creating a shadow. Higher elevation = larger, softer shadow. Like picking up a card from a table.

concepts.name: CSS filters
concepts.definition: filter: blur(4px), grayscale(100%), brightness(150%), saturate(200%), hue-rotate(180deg). Apply visual effects to any element. Can combine: filter: blur(2px) brightness(120%).
concepts.analogy: Instagram filters for CSS — blur, black-and-white, brightness, all applied in code.

concepts.name: Glassmorphism with backdrop-filter
concepts.definition: backdrop-filter: blur(10px) blurs content BEHIND the element. Combined with rgba() semi-transparent backgrounds and subtle borders, creates the modern "frosted glass" effect.
concepts.analogy: Looking through frosted glass — you can see shapes and colors behind it, but they're blurred and softened.

understand.keyTakeaway: box-shadow creates depth, filter transforms elements visually, backdrop-filter blurs what's behind an element. Together they create modern, polished designs.

build.goal: Apply filters to images and create a glassmorphism card
build.hint: backdrop-filter needs the -webkit- prefix for Safari. Always include both!

challenge.title: Build a Modern Login Card
challenge.mission: Create a beautiful login form with glassmorphism, shadows, and filter effects.
requirements: Glassmorphism card on a gradient background
Input fields with focus glow effects
challenge.starterCode:
Welcome Back

Don't have an account? Sign up
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add social login buttons (Google, GitHub) with icon styling!

capabilities: Create depth with box-shadow
Use multiple layered shadows
Apply CSS filters (blur, grayscale, brightness)
Create glassmorphism effects with backdrop-filter
Build modern login forms
Combine shadows and filters for visual impact

bridge.preview: In Lesson 4.6, you'll combine EVERYTHING you've learned across all 4 modules to build a complete, professional, polished portfolio website. This is your CSS capstone project!

accomplishments: Create something you're proud to share

do.title: Build your complete portfolio
steps: Plan your color scheme (primary, secondary, neutrals) using coolors.co
Choose 3-6 projects to showcase
Write your "about" section
Create a new file: final-portfolio.html
Build section by section: Nav → Hero → About → Projects → Contact → Footer
Test at mobile, tablet, and desktop widths
Replace ALL placeholder content with YOUR information
do.code:
<!-- This is your capstone project. Use everything you've learned! -->

<!-- Required sections: -->
<!-- 1. Sticky, responsive navigation -->
<!-- 2. Hero with gradient background + CTA -->
<!-- 3. About section with skills badges -->
<!-- 4. Projects gallery (responsive grid + hover effects) -->
<!-- 5. Contact section -->
<!-- 6. Footer -->

<!-- Required techniques checklist: -->
<!-- Module 1: Colors, gradients, typography, spacing, borders, shadows -->
<!-- Module 2: Flexbox, Grid, sticky positioning, display properties -->
<!-- Module 3: Media queries, mobile-first, responsive images, clamp() -->
<!-- Module 4: Transitions, hover animations, transforms, CSS variables -->

<!-- Start with the portfolio template from Lesson 3.6 -->
<!-- Then enhance it with everything from Module 4! -->
do.result: You built a complete, professional portfolio combining every CSS technique from the entire curriculum!
do.proofRequired: Screenshots at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths showing your finished portfolio.

concepts.name: Bringing it all together
concepts.definition: A professional website uses CSS Foundations (colors, typography, spacing), Layout (Flexbox, Grid), Responsive Design (media queries, clamp()), and Advanced Styling (animations, transforms, variables) working in harmony.
concepts.analogy: A full orchestra — each section plays a different instrument but they all work together to create one song.

concepts.name: Design system approach
concepts.definition: Define all your values (colors, spacing, typography, shadows, transitions) as CSS variables in :root. Then use them consistently everywhere. This makes your site cohesive and easy to maintain.
concepts.analogy: A recipe book for your entire website — consistent ingredients, consistent results.

concepts.name: Progressive enhancement
concepts.definition: Start with solid HTML structure, add core CSS styling, then layer on animations and advanced effects. The site should work without JavaScript or fancy effects.
concepts.analogy: Building a house: foundation first, then walls, then paint, then furniture, then decorations.

understand.keyTakeaway: Professional websites combine multiple CSS techniques working together. Master each one individually, then combine them into cohesive projects.

build.goal: Enhance your portfolio with Module 4 techniques
build.hint: Start with your Lesson 3.6 portfolio, then add CSS variables, transitions, hover transforms, and glassmorphism effects.

challenge.title: Make It Production-Ready
challenge.mission: Polish your portfolio to a level you'd be proud to show employers or clients.
requirements: All placeholder content replaced with YOUR real information
At least 3 real projects with descriptions
Custom color scheme matching your personal brand
Working on mobile, tablet, and desktop
Smooth hover effects and transitions on all interactive elements
CSS variables used for consistent theming
Accessible (proper contrast, focus states, semantic HTML)
challenge.starterCode:
<!-- Use your portfolio from Lesson 3.6 as the starting point -->
<!-- Add these Module 4 enhancements: -->
<!-- 1. CSS variables for your color scheme -->
<!-- 2. Smooth transitions on all interactive elements -->
<!-- 3. Transform hover effects on project cards -->
<!-- 4. Box-shadow depth system -->
<!-- 5. Optional: glassmorphism, dark mode toggle -->
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add a dark mode toggle with localStorage persistence, animated hamburger menu for mobile, or scroll-triggered animations!

capabilities: Build complete, professional portfolio websites from scratch
Combine CSS Foundations, Layout, Responsive Design, and Advanced Styling
Create cohesive designs with CSS variable design systems
Implement responsive layouts that work on any device
Add professional animations, transitions, and hover effects
Use modern CSS techniques (clamp, grid auto-fit, backdrop-filter)
Write maintainable, scalable CSS
Design with visual hierarchy, depth, and polish

bridge.preview: Congratulations — you've completed the entire CSS curriculum! You're now ready to learn JavaScript and make your websites interactive, or dive into React to build complex applications.
